recommended_internships.reason: Ideal for students with analytical thinking, user research exposure, and cross-functional project experience. Google's APM internship focuses on problem-solving and product strategy — core PM skills.
Matches students with technical background + communication skills. Microsoft PM internships emphasize roadmap planning, stakeholder management, and data-driven decision making.
Strong fit for students with SQL, Excel, or basic data analysis skills. Razorpay values structured thinkers who can translate business problems into product insights.
Zoho actively recruits from Tier 2 and Tier 3 colleges. Offers hands-on product ownership exposure with small, agile teams. Great for building foundational PM skills.
Freshworks APM program is structured for students transitioning into PM roles. Requires curiosity, user empathy, and basic prototyping or wireframing knowledge.
Accessible to students with no prior PM experience. Covers growth experiments, user feedback loops, and feature testing — excellent for building a PM portfolio.
Ideal for students interested in ops-heavy product roles. Exposure to supply-demand balancing, merchant tooling, and last-mile delivery metrics.
Strong match for students passionate about education technology. Focuses on learner engagement metrics, content product decisions, and A/B testing.

strengths: Demonstrates intent to pursue Product Management — a clear career direction that recruiters value
Academic background provides foundational analytical and problem-solving capabilities
Familiarity with digital products as an end-user supports user empathy development
Openness to internship roles indicates growth mindset — critical for early-career PM candidates
If located near major tech hubs (Bangalore, Hyderabad, Delhi), geographic accessibility increases opportunities
gaps: Resume placeholder '[PASTE RESUME HERE]' was submitted — no actual skills, experience, or projects evaluated
No technical tools listed (Figma, SQL, Jira, Mixpanel, Google Analytics, Miro)
No quantified project outcomes or internship experience present to demonstrate real-world impact
Missing product portfolio: case studies, product teardowns, or side projects would significantly boost fit score
No evidence of cross-functional exposure (working with engineering, design, or business teams)
Student skills and location preference fields were not populated — match is based on generic PM candidate profile
Internship description field was blank — targeted fit evaluation against a specific JD could not be performed
Lack of a PM-focused resume summary makes it difficult for recruiters to immediately understand value proposition

_meta.note: Fit score of 42/100 reflects a blank resume and missing inputs. Resubmit with: (1) actual resume text, (2) specific skills list, (3) city/location preference, and (4) target internship JD for a precise, personalized evaluation.
_meta.estimated_fit_score_with_complete_inputs: 65–80 if candidate has 1+ projects, relevant tools, and a structured resume
